audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Herod's fear, John the Baptist's death, Jesus' withdrawal, compassion, feeding five thousand, twelve baskets, walking on sea, Peter on waters, little trust, worship/prostration, and Gennesaret healings.

verses.restored: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the report about Jesus.
He said to his servants, This is John the Baptist; he has been raised from the dead, and because of this the powers are at work in him.
For Herod had seized John, bound him, and put him in prison because of Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip.
For John had been saying to him, It is not lawful for you to have her.
Though he wanted to kill him, he feared the crowd, because they held him as a prophet.
When Herod's birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced in the midst and pleased Herod.
Because of this, with an oath, he promised to give her whatever she might ask.
Prompted by her mother, she said, Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.
The king was grieved, but because of the oaths and those reclining with him, he commanded it to be given.
He sent and had John beheaded in the prison.
His head was brought on a platter and given to the girl, and she brought it to her mother.
His disciples came, took the body, and buried him; then they went and reported to Jesus.
When Jesus heard, he withdrew from there in a boat to a deserted place by himself. The crowds heard and followed him on foot from the cities.
Coming out, he saw a great crowd and was moved with compassion over them, and he healed their sick.
When evening came, the disciples came to him, saying, This place is deserted and the hour has passed; release the crowds so they may go into the villages and buy food for themselves.
But Jesus said to them, They have no need to go away; you give them something to eat.
They said to him, We have nothing here except five loaves and two fish.
He said, Bring them here to me.
Commanding the crowds to recline on the grass, he took the five loaves and two fish, looked up to heaven, blessed, broke the loaves, and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples to the crowds.
They all ate and were filled, and they took up what remained of the broken pieces, twelve baskets full.
Those eating were about five thousand men, besides women and children.
Immediately he compelled the disciples to enter the boat and go before him to the other side, while he released the crowds.
After releasing the crowds, he went up into the mountain by himself to pray. When evening came, he was there alone.
The boat was already many stadia from the land, being tormented by waves, for the wind was against them.
In the fourth watch of the night, he came toward them, walking on the sea.
When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, It is a ghost/appearance, and they cried out from fear.
Immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, Take courage; I am. Do not fear.
Peter answered him, Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the waters.
He said, Come. Peter got down from the boat and walked on the waters and came toward Jesus.
But seeing the strong wind, he was afraid; beginning to sink, he cried out, Lord, save me.
Immediately Jesus stretched out his hand, took hold of him, and said to him, One of little trust, why did you doubt/divide?
When they got into the boat, the wind ceased.
Those in the boat bowed/prostrated before him, saying, Truly you are Son of the Divine.
Crossing over, they came to land at Gennesaret.
When the men of that place recognized him, they sent into all that surrounding region and brought to him all who were sick.
They pleaded with him that they might only touch the fringe of his garment; and as many as touched were thoroughly saved/healed.
